what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG icon showing a circle with a diagonal slash from upper left to lower right.
observations: Uses an SVG viewBox of 0 0 24 24 with matching width and height attributes.
Draws a stroked circle centered at 12,12 with radius 9.
Draws a diagonal path from 6,6 to 18,18.
Uses no fill and currentColor stroke so the icon color inherits from its context.
Sets stroke width to 1.5 with round line caps and round line joins.
standouts: Minimal two-shape construction: one circle and one slash.
Geometry reads as a no, none, blocked, or prohibited symbol.
conventions: Follows a common 24x24 icon grid convention.
Follows stroke-only SVG icon styling with fill="none" and stroke="currentColor".
Uses round stroke caps and joins for softened line endings.
Keeps all geometry inline in the SVG without external styles or scripts.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present, so accessible naming must come from usage context if needed.
notes: A compact reusable prohibition-style SVG icon made from a circle and diagonal slash.
questions: Should this asset include built-in accessibility metadata, or is labeling handled by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the apparent filename purpose: it renders a no/prohibition symbol.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a small reusable UI icon for representing absence, blocking, denial, or a negative state while inheriting color from surrounding UI.
contribution: Contributes a simple currentColor prohibition glyph that a fused system would keep as a reusable negative-state or blocked-action visual asset.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 stroke-only SVG icon composed of a circle and diagonal slash representing a no or prohibition symbol.
intention_for_embedding: This asset was for showing a compact negative, blocked, or disallowed state icon that adapts to the current text color.
